text: Handling deadlines effectively is an essential skill in both academic and professional settings. Good time management helps you break large tasks into smaller steps, making them less overwhelming. Prioritization is equally important: identify which tasks are urgent or carry the most impact, and focus on those first. Sometimes, managing scope means saying no to extra work or adjusting your goals when a project becomes too large. Finally, communication with your team or supervisor is crucial—if you foresee a delay, inform them early rather than waiting until the last moment. By combining these strategies, you can reduce stress and deliver quality work on time.
